ocr.text: The analysis presented in the table provides a comprehensive overview of the morphological characteristics of Rhinella marina
across various size ranges, specifically focusing on the mean standard uptake value (Mean SUL) and mean mass.
PrWC (n = 84)  PrBC (n = 83)  PuWC (n = 47)  p-value
The data is categorized into three distinct size intervals: [0, 1.5[, [1.5, 2.7[, and [2.7, 5.5[ mm, each accompanied by the number
of specimens (n) and their respective percentages. Notably, the table highlights that within the smallest size category, 14
individuals were recorded, representing 16.7% of the total sample, while the corresponding confidence interval (CI) for this
metric ranged from 10.2 to 26.1.

Furthermore, the mean mass was observed to vary significantly across the size categories, indicating potential implications for
understanding the growth patterns and ecological dynamics of the species. Such information is crucial for researchers and
conservationists, as it aids in assessing the health and viability of Rhinella marina populations in their natural habitats. The
detailed statistical representation facilitates a deeper understanding of size-related trends, which are essential for informed
management strategies.
